title: Hierarchy and Interdependence of Boolean Algebra Properties
description: A visual map showing how fundamental Boolean theorems (Single Variable, Commutative, Associative, Distributive) are built upon to form more complex properties (De Morgan's, Absorption, etc.) and how they all connect back to the core axioms.
elements: Single Variable Theorems (Identity, Null, Idempotency, etc.)
relationships: Single Variable Theorems are direct consequences of the axioms
Commutative, Associative, and Distributive Laws are multi-variable extensions
De Morgan's, Absorption, and Consensus Theorems are proven by applying the foundational laws
